segments.text: "After David finished speaking with Saul, Jonathan became great friends with David. He loved David as he loved himself.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: "From that time on Jonathan had David work for him and would not let him go back home.
segments.speaker: Samuel

segments.text: "Jonathan made a solemn agreement with David because he loved him as he loved himself.
segments.speaker: Samuel

segments.text: "Jonathan took off the robe he was wearing and gave it to David, together with his tunic, his sword, his bow, and his belt.
"David was successful in doing everything Saul asked him to do, so Saul made him an officer in the army. This pleased everyone, including Saul's other officers.
segments.speaker: Samuel

segments.text: "When the soldiers returned home after David had killed the Philistine, the women of all the towns of Israel came out singing and dancing to meet King Saul, happily celebrating with tambourines and musical instruments.
segments.speaker: Samuel

segments.text: "As they danced the women sang,
Saul has killed his thousands, and David his tens of thousands.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: "What they were singing made Saul very angry as he didn't think it was right. He said to himself,
segments.speaker: Goliath

segments.text: They've given David credit for killing tens of thousands, but only thousands to me. All that's left is to give him the kingdom!
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: "From that time on Saul viewed David with suspicion.
segments.speaker: Goliath

segments.text: "The following day an evil spirit from God came on Saul with power, and he was ranting inside the house while David played the harp as he regularly did. Saul happened to be holding a spear,
segments.speaker: Samuel

segments.text: "and he threw it at David, saying to himself,
segments.speaker: Samuel

segments.text: I'll pin David to the wall.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: But David managed to escape him twice.
"Saul was afraid of David, because the Lord was with David, but he had given up on Saul.
"So Saul sent David away and made him a commander of a thousand soldiers, leading them out and back as part of the army.
"David was very successful in everything he did, because the Lord was with him.
segments.speaker: Samuel

segments.text: "When Saul saw how successful David was, he was even more afraid of him.
segments.speaker: Saul's servants

segments.text: "But everyone in Israel and Judah loved David, because of his leadership in the army.
segments.speaker: Samuel

segments.text: "One day Saul told David,
segments.speaker: Samuel

segments.text: Here's my oldest daughter Merab. I will give her to you in marriage, but only if you prove to me you're a brave warrior and fight the battles of the Lord.
segments.speaker: Saul

segments.text: For Saul was thinking,
I don't need to be the one to kill him—let the Philistines do it!
segments.speaker: One day Saul

segments.text: But who am I, and what status does my family have in Israel, for me to become the son-in-law of the king?
segments.speaker: Samuel

segments.text: David replied.
"However, when the time came to give Merab, Saul's daughter, to David, she was given in marriage to Adriel of Meholah instead.
segments.speaker: Saul

segments.text: "Meanwhile Saul's daughter Michal had fallen in love with David, and when Saul was told, he was happy about it.
segments.speaker: Saul

segments.text: I'll give her to David,
segments.speaker: Samuel

segments.text: Saul thought.
She can be the bait so the Philistines can trap him.
segments.speaker: Saul

segments.text: So Saul said to David,
This is the second time you can become my son-in-law.
segments.speaker: Saul

segments.text: "Saul gave these instructions to his servants,
segments.speaker: Saul

segments.text: Talk with David in private and tell him, ‘Look, the king is very happy with you, and all of us love you. Why not become the king's son-in-law?’
segments.speaker: Samuel

segments.text: "Saul's servants spoke privately to David, but he replied,
segments.speaker: Samuel

segments.text: Do you think it's nothing to become the king's son-in-law? I'm a poor man, and I'm not important.
segments.speaker: Saul's servants

segments.text: "When Saul's servants explained to him what David had said,
segments.speaker: Saul

segments.text: "Saul told them,
Tell David, ‘The only dowry the king wants for the bride is one hundred foreskins of dead Philistines as a way of taking revenge on his enemies.’
segments.speaker: Saul

segments.text: Saul's plan was to have David be killed by the Philistines.
"When the servants reported what the king had said back to David, he was happy to become the king's son-in-law. While there was still time,
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: "David set off with his men and killed two hundred Philistines, and brought back their foreskins. They counted them all out before the king so that David could become the king's son-in-law. So Saul gave him his daughter Michal in marriage.
segments.speaker: The soldiers

segments.text: "Saul realized that the Lord was with David and that his daughter Michal was in love with David,
segments.speaker: Eliab

segments.text: "and so he became even more afraid of David, and was David's enemy for the rest of his life.
"Whenever the Philistine commanders attacked, David was more successful in battle than all of Saul's officers, so that his reputation grew rapidly.
